stemHtml: What is the difference between an LNAV and an LP approach to the same runway?

options.a: There is no significant difference as both use GNSS as reference, so the pilot may choose the one with lowest minima.
options.b: LP is a 3D approach, similar to VNAV.
options.c: LNAV offers both lateral and vertical guidance.
options.d: LP follows the Localiser Performance standards, which requires additional equipment on board the aircraft, beyond the standard GNSS.
